body.title: Utility closet rack architecture: modular services with standard interface
body.result: RackModule base class + Rack registry. Health checks, lifecycle (start/stop/heartbeat), thread-safe, get_rack() singleton. 22 tests.
body.description:
Establish the utility closet as a rackmount-style service host. Each module is an independent shelf — own lifecycle, own tests, standard registration interface, communicates via comms channels.

## Vision
- Igor = brain (cognition, memory graph, engrams). UC = body (infrastructure, periphery, I/O).
- Boundary test: if it requires graph-native cognition, it's Igor. If it's a generalized service, it's a UC rack module.
- Igor connects as a client. Never needs to know if a message came from web UI, Discord, Matter, or a minion — it's all channel messages.

## UC core (the rack frame)
- Module registration on startup (extend existing agent registration pattern)
- Health endpoint per module
- Shared comms bus for inter-module communication
- Web UI for visibility into rack state

## Rack modules (shelves)
- **Comms** — channel routing, message envelopes, notifications (T-uc-comms-module)
- **Web UI** — Svelte frontend, WebSocket hub (T-uc-channel-tabs-redesign)
- **Matter** — home automation sensors/actuators (GH#185)
- **Certs/TLS** — domain management, Tailscale (T-uc-cert-domain-migration, T-tailscale-cert-restore)
- **System Proxy** — hardware metrics, resource monitoring (already built)
- **Future** — minion launcher, backup, research corpus, etc.

## Standard module interface
- Register with UC core on startup (name, version, health URL)
- Expose health/status endpoint
- Communicate through comms channels (not direct function calls between modules)
- Independently testable — unit tests run against just that module
- Can restart/update without taking down the whole closet

## Related
- T-uc-channel-tabs-redesign — web UI module
- T-uc-named-channels (#403) — feeds into comms module
- T-uc-base-class (#402) — may evolve into the standard module interface
- D335 — original utility closet decision

## Depends on
- T-uc-comms-module (comms bus is the backbone — build that first)
